title: Software Engineer Resume Example
description: Sample lines, section order, and ATS keywords for developer resumes in India — product, services, and startups.
sampleSummary: Software Engineer with 3+ years building backend services in Java and Spring Boot. Shipped APIs serving 2M+ daily requests; focused on reliability, observability, and clean delivery in agile squads.
sampleBullets: Owned payment reconciliation service; cut p99 latency by 28% via query tuning and caching (Spring Boot, Redis, PostgreSQL).
Led migration of legacy monolith module to REST microservice; zero Sev-1 incidents in first 90 days post-cutover.
Mentored 2 junior engineers on code review, testing, and production readiness practices.
indiaContext: Campus and off-campus hiring in India often combines DSA rounds with system design (for experienced roles) and deep project discussion. Your resume should back every stack claim with employers, timeframes, and measurable outcomes so recruiters can match you to the right panel.
aiEraNote: Copilot-style coding assistants and ChatGPT can speed up learning and boilerplate, but Indian tech interviews still test problem-solving, system design (mid/senior), and code you can explain. List AI/ML frameworks only when you have real projects — recruiters discount buzzwords without shipped work.

earlyCareer: One primary language + basics of HTML/CSS or REST
midLevel: Your stack’s frameworks (e.g. Spring Boot, React, Django)
senior: Architecture patterns — microservices, events, data stores

seniorityNote: Junior and mid-level engineers should foreground shipped features, honest stack depth, and measurable impact (latency, reliability, adoption). Senior and staff profiles should highlight architecture trade-offs, production ownership, and mentoring—only when those were truly part of your mandate.
tips: Open with a tight summary: years of experience, primary stack, and one proof point (scale, reliability, or product impact).
For each role, use 3–5 bullets: what you built, technologies, and a metric or clear outcome — avoid task-only lines like "worked on modules".
List skills truthfully. Keyword-stuffing tools you only used once hurts trust and interviews.
Fresher or <1 YOE: put 2 strong projects (problem, stack, your part, result) and link GitHub if quality is good.
Senior roles: mention ownership (on-call, design, mentoring) only if real; add system design keywords when they match actual work.
mistakesToAvoid: Ten-page CVs or tiny fonts to fit everything — recruiters scan in seconds; two pages max for most Indian tech roles.
Buzzwords without proof ("AI/ML enthusiast") when experience is unrelated.
Employer names lost inside complex tables or graphics that confuse ATS parsers.
Hobby sections that eat space without supporting the engineering story.
atsIntro: Indian tech JDs mix English titles (Software Engineer, SDE, Backend Developer). Align your headline with the posting family without inventing titles you never held.
atsChecklist: Mirror the job title line once in summary or headline if it matches your real role.
Spell frameworks and tools in full at least once (e.g. "JavaScript" not only "JS").
Quantify latency, uptime, adoption, or defect reduction where you can verify.
Use a single-column, text-first PDF — no text boxes or multi-column tricks.

title: Fresher Resume Example
description: Education-first layout, honest projects, and keywords for campus drives and entry-level roles on Naukri and LinkedIn.
sampleSummary: B.Tech Computer Science graduate (2025) seeking a software engineering trainee role. Strong fundamentals in data structures, OOP, and web development; built two full-stack academic projects with measurable outcomes.
sampleBullets: Expense-split app (React, Node.js, MongoDB): 4-person team; owned auth and REST APIs; demo shortlisted in college hackathon.
Data structures lab mentor: helped 30+ peers with problem-solving patterns; improved average assignment scores.
Summer internship — ABC Infotech: automated weekly Excel reports with Python; saved ~4 hours/week for the team.
indiaContext: Mass recruiting and campus drives reward one crisp page: clear degree, CGPA or percentage if strong, projects with your real contribution, and skills that match the companies you target. Avoid long "career objective" essays.
aiEraNote: It is fine to use AI to brainstorm bullets or study — interviews will still ask you to explain your projects and write code on the spot. Never list "prompt engineering" or vague AI skills unless the role is ML/GenAI; focus on languages, Git, and projects you can defend.

earlyCareer: Git basics & one structured project repo
Core language + simple web or API stack from coursework
Excel or Sheets for internship reporting tasks
Documentation & debugging habits over tool hoarding
midLevel: Deepen one stack (e.g. MERN or Java/Spring) with 2+ shipped projects
Internship tools from real use — not certificate spam
Optional: LeetCode patterns aligned to target companies
senior: Not typical for freshers — if pivoting with experience, show transferable tools from prior domain
Leadership in college tech clubs / OSS only if substantiated
Certifications that match JD (cloud, security) when earned

seniorityNote: Campus and entry-level hiring reads one profile: education, projects, and internships first—tone stays trainee, not executive. If you have serious freelance or part-time delivery, add a short experience block with dates and outcomes without overstating title seniority.
tips: Order: Education → Projects/Internships → Skills → Certifications. Put the strongest proof early.
Use a 2-line objective or summary: role you want + 1 strength (stack, internship, or rank).
Projects: problem → stack → your role → outcome. One bullet each is enough if dense.
Skills: comma-separated or grouped (Languages / Web / DB) — easy for ATS and humans.
Mention coursework or certifications only when they support the roles you apply for.
mistakesToAvoid: Half-page objectives filled with clichés ("hard-working, quick learner" alone).
Padding experience — fake internships or inflated titles backfire in BGV.
Cluttered templates that break when uploaded to Taleo or company portals.
Listing every school competition unless it strengthens the narrative.
atsIntro: Entry-level postings use titles like Graduate Engineer Trainee, GET, Analyst Trainee, or "Fresher — Software". Reuse the exact family from the JD when truthful.
atsChecklist: Spell "internship" and degree names consistently.
Include tools you actually used in projects, not a generic skill cloud.
Keep file name professional: Firstname_Lastname_Resume.pdf.
One page for most freshers unless you have two strong internships plus multiple projects.

title: Data Analyst Resume Example
description: Dashboard-focused sample bullets, tool stacks, and ATS terms for analysts in IT, banking, e-commerce, and GCCs.
sampleSummary: Data Analyst with 4 years translating business questions into dashboards and recurring reports. Strong SQL, Excel, and Power BI; comfortable partnering with ops and finance stakeholders in fast-paced environments.
sampleBullets: Built executive sales dashboard in Power BI; reduced ad-hoc Excel requests by ~40% within one quarter.
Owned monthly revenue reconciliation SQL pipeline; cut manual errors by standardizing joins and QA checks.
Partnered with marketing on funnel analysis; identified drop-off stage contributing to 12% improvement in lead quality.
indiaContext: Analyst hiring in India spans IT services GCCs, banking and NBFC reporting teams, e-commerce, and growth-stage startups. Most recruiters expect credible SQL, strong Excel, and at least one viz tool in production—plus bullets that show business questions answered, not a shallow list of every BI certificate.
aiEraNote: AI assistants can draft SQL or summarize data — employers still expect you to validate numbers, know your joins, and explain dashboards in interviews. Mention Copilot/Notebook LM style workflows only if they reflect how you actually work; core signals remain SQL, Excel depth, and one BI stack.

earlyCareer: One viz tool: Power BI or Tableau
midLevel: Python or R for repeatable analysis — only if used
ETL familiarity (dbt, SSIS, or similar) when truthful
senior: Coaching juniors — less about listing every BI toy

seniorityNote: Early-career analysts win on credible SQL, Excel, and one BI tool backed by internship or project proof. Mid-level resumes should tie bullets to recurring stakeholders and decisions influenced. Senior profiles add metric ownership, data QA, and mentoring teammates—when accurate.
tips: Minimum credible story: SQL + spreadsheets + one viz tool (Power BI, Tableau, Looker). Add Python/R only if you use them.
Frame bullets as question → analysis → decision or impact (time saved, accuracy, adoption).
Certifications help early career; name them with issuer and year.
For freshers, highlight academic or Kaggle-style projects with clear metrics.
Avoid "passionate about data" without evidence — show datasets, stakeholders, or outcomes.
mistakesToAvoid: Listing ten BI tools at beginner level — depth beats breadth.
Vanity charts with no business outcome ("pretty dashboard" with no user or decision).
Copy-pasting engineering deployment bullets that are not analytics work.
Ignoring Excel — many India roles still lean heavily on advanced Excel.
atsIntro: Analyst JDs emphasize SQL, Excel, dashboards, KPIs, and stakeholder communication. Lead with those when accurate; add domain (fintech, e-comm) if you have it.
atsChecklist: Use "SQL" in skills and at least one bullet with a concrete use case.
Name the viz tool you used in production or internships, not only coursework.
Quantify report cycle time, error reduction, or dashboard adoption.
Keep marketing campaign jargon out unless you truly support marketing analytics.

title: Product Manager Resume Example
description: Outcome-first PM resume patterns for SaaS, fintech, and digital products — metrics, discovery, and stakeholder impact.
sampleSummary: Product Manager (B2B SaaS) with 5 years owning roadmap and discovery for billing and integrations. Shipped features that improved net retention by 3 pts and cut onboarding time for mid-market customers.
sampleBullets: Ran discovery with 15+ customer calls/quarter; prioritized backlog with RICE; shipped invoicing workflow used by 200+ accounts.
Partnered with engineering and design on API roadmap; balanced tech debt vs revenue features with exec-visible tradeoffs.
Defined success metrics and dashboards; presented monthly outcomes to leadership and CS leadership.
indiaContext: Product roles in India include domestic startups, GCC product teams, and tech firms with platform ownership. Panels care about outcomes—retention, revenue, adoption—and clear ownership of discovery and prioritization, not a calendar of meetings. Align your title with the JD (PM vs PO vs APM) when it matches your real scope.
aiEraNote: AI tools accelerate PRD drafts and research synthesis — hiring managers still judge judgment, prioritization, and stakeholder stories. Listing "ChatGPT" as a skill adds little; emphasize discovery, analytics, and outcomes you owned.

earlyCareer: Jira / Linear / Azure DevOps for backlog hygiene
Basic product analytics (GA4, Mixpanel, Amplitude) when exposed
midLevel: SQL or BI read access for self-serve metrics
Customer interview & survey tools (Zoom, Typeform, UserTesting)
senior: Portfolio strategy, pricing/partnerships exposure when real
Mentoring PMs — less about individual tickets

seniorityNote: Associate PMs should show structured discovery, analytics literacy, and shipped experiments or features tied to learning. Mid-level PMs need retention, revenue, or adoption metrics linked to bets they owned. Senior PMs signal portfolio judgment, exec-ready narratives, and cross-org rollouts—without borrowing engineering delivery wins.
tips: Summary: domain (B2B/B2C), years, and one metric (retention, revenue, adoption, NPS).
Bullets: problem insight → decision → launch → measurable outcome — not meeting lists.
Clarify PM vs project coordinator: ownership of problem, metrics, and tradeoffs.
Tools: Jira/Linear, analytics (Amplitude, Mixpanel, GA), research — only what you use.
If APM/fresher pivot, highlight structured projects, case comps, or MBA capstone with metrics.
mistakesToAvoid: "Responsible for PRDs" with no impact or user evidence.
Claiming engineering delivery you did not own as PM wins.
Buzzword soup ("synergy", "vision") without specifics.
Pure implementation bullets copied from engineers — recruiters want product outcomes.
atsIntro: India JDs mix Product Manager, Product Owner, and APM. Use the title that matches your accountability; PO often implies backlog depth with one squad.
atsChecklist: Tie each major bullet to a user or business metric.
Name analytics or research methods you actually used.
Separate true PM scope from pure project tracking.
Avoid developer-only keywords dominating the page unless you are technical PM with that balance.

title: Marketing Manager Resume Example
description: Channel clarity, campaign metrics, and ATS language for digital, brand, and growth marketers in India.
sampleSummary: Marketing Manager with 6 years across performance and brand in consumer e-commerce. Scaled paid acquisition while improving blended ROAS; led seasonal campaigns with cross-functional creative and ops teams.
sampleBullets: Managed ₹4Cr+ annual paid media (Meta + Google); improved ROAS by 18% YoY through creative testing and audience restructuring.
Launched monsoon campaign: 22% lift in category sessions; coordinated influencers, CRM, and on-site merchandising.
Built weekly performance reporting cadence for leadership; shortened decision cycles on budget shifts.
indiaContext: Digital and performance marketing in India is dominated by D2C, e-commerce marketplaces, and BFSI app growth. In-house and agency roles both value channel ownership and rupee-level spend discipline—portfolios and metrics should show impact on revenue or CAC, not only creative awards.
aiEraNote: AI copy and creative generators are common — differentiate with channel expertise, experiment results, and rupee accountability. Resumes should highlight platforms you operated (Meta, Google) and outcomes, not generic "AI marketing" hype.

earlyCareer: Meta Ads Manager & Google Ads basics
midLevel: CRM/lifecycle tools (Clevertap, MoEngage, WebEngage) for retention roles

seniorityNote: Coordinators and executives emphasize execution cadence, creative tests, and rupee discipline at different scales. Mid-level marketers should show owned budgets or channels with funnel outcomes; senior profiles add agency governance, mixed brand–performance strategy, and leadership scope—only when real.
tips: State channel ownership early: performance, brand, growth, CRM, or mix.
Every campaign bullet should include audience, channel, and outcome (reach, CAC, ROAS, conversion).
Agency experience: clarify client brands, budgets, and your role vs vendor execution.
Stack: Google Ads, Meta Ads Manager, SEO tools, analytics — match reality.
Senior roles: mention team size, budget scope, and P&L or growth accountability when true.
mistakesToAvoid: Only vanity metrics (likes, impressions) with no revenue or funnel link.
Vague "handled social media" without scale or results.
Omitting budget or scale signals when applying for lead roles.
Using sales quota language that confuses marketing scope.
atsIntro: Digital marketing JDs in India emphasize performance marketing, ROAS, funnels, and attribution. Brand roles may stress reach and creative excellence — tune keywords to the posting.
atsChecklist: Align headline with JD: growth vs brand vs performance.
Include one before/after or YoY metric per major campaign.
Name primary ad platforms and analytics tools you operated.
Do not reuse HR or sales metrics that are not marketing outcomes.

title: Sales Executive Resume Example
description: Quota, pipeline, and CRM-focused resume lines for B2B, retail, insurance, and channel sales in India.
sampleSummary: Enterprise sales executive with 7 years in B2B IT services and SaaS. Comfortable with long-cycle deals, multi-threaded stakeholders, and forecast discipline in Salesforce.
sampleBullets: Exceeded annual quota 3 consecutive years (112%–125%); avg deal size ₹45L+ in mid-market accounts.
Opened 14 new logos in FY24 in manufacturing vertical; partnered with presales on POC-to-close motion.
Maintained 4.2× pipeline coverage; improved forecast accuracy for region to leadership targets.
indiaContext: Sales hiring spans IT services, SaaS, manufacturing, insurance, and retail. B2B panels expect CRM hygiene, pipeline language, and honest quota or revenue signals (use % or bands if numbers are sensitive). Field vs inside sales and territory should be obvious from your CV.
aiEraNote: AI outreach and dialer assistants exist — credibility still comes from quota proof, CRM accuracy, and deal storytelling. List sales engagement AI only if your employer adopted it and you operated it daily.

midLevel: CPQ or proposal tools if enterprise
LinkedIn Sales Navigator / ZoomInfo-style research when used

seniorityNote: SMB and inside sellers highlight pipeline hygiene, conversion discipline, and CRM accuracy; enterprise sellers stress multi-threaded pursuits, deal bands, and forecast reliability. Senior sales leaders add coverage design and team outcomes—avoid marketing ROAS language unless that remit was truly yours.
tips: Summary: domain (B2B/B2C), motion (hunter/farmer), average deal or segment.
Bullets: quota %, revenue or ACV bands, new logos, cycle time — use ranges if confidentiality matters.
Name CRM (Salesforce, Zoho) and methodologies (MEDDIC, SPIN) only if you truly use them.
Inside sales vs field: make travel and territory clear.
Retail or telecom: emphasize footfall, conversions, attachments, or regional rank where allowed.
mistakesToAvoid: Only activity metrics (calls, visits) with no business outcome.
Publishing confidential client revenue; use % or banded figures.
Mixing marketing optimization language that is not sales ownership.
Missing digital sales tools if role is hybrid (LinkedIn Sales Navigator, etc.).
atsIntro: Sales JDs vary: B2B enterprise, SMB, channel, retail. Mirror the motion and keywords (account management, new business, BD) that match your experience.
atsChecklist: Include at least one quota or ranking outcome if policy allows.
Separate farming (renewal, expansion) from hunting if both apply.
Spell CRM correctly and tie it to forecasting or pipeline hygiene.
Avoid campaign ROAS language unless you truly carry a hybrid remit.

title: HR Manager Resume Example
description: TA, HR ops, and people metrics — sample lines and keywords for corporates and scaling Indian startups.
sampleSummary: HR Manager with 8 years across talent acquisition and HR operations in IT services. Scaled hiring for 400+ annual offers; improved offer-to-join ratio with candidate experience and process fixes.
sampleBullets: Led TA team of 6; met aggressive hiring plan for digital skills during 40% YoY headcount growth.
Redesigned onboarding journey with IT and admin; cut Day-1 friction tickets by ~30%.
Partnered with leadership on engagement pulse; rolled out manager toolkit for 1:1s and feedback.
indiaContext: Indian enterprises and scaling startups hire TA and HR generalists with strong volume hiring, statutory awareness, and HRIS/payroll familiarity. GCC-style HR roles may mirror global process language—still anchor outcomes (joiners, TAT, engagement) in facts you can defend.
aiEraNote: AI sourcing and chatbots assist TA — shortlisting still needs human judgment on fit and compliance. Emphasize HRIS, ATS, and metrics you owned; avoid claiming strategic AI transformation unless that was your mandate.

earlyCareer: ATS basics (Naukri RMS, Lever, Greenhouse) when exposed
midLevel: Offer & onboarding workflows with payroll partners
Dashboards for TAT and joiner analytics
senior: Policy & ER complexity with compliance awareness

seniorityNote: Coordinators spotlight scheduling, ATS hygiene, and candidate experience basics. Mid-level TA leads show hiring volume, TAT, and offer-to-join wins. Senior HRBP or HR heads emphasize workforce planning with business leaders, ER awareness, and engagement programmes anchored in metrics—without overstating legal authority.
tips: Clarify spine: TA, HRBP, comp & benefits, or generalist — recruiters match fast.
Quantify: offers/month, TAT, offer-to-join, attrition support, engagement scores.
Tools: HRIS (Workday, Darwinbox, Zoho People), ATS (Naukri RMS, LinkedIn Recruiter), payroll partners.
Compliance-aware language for India context (statutory, POSH training) without giving legal advice.
HRBP: business partnering bullets with BU leaders and workforce planning.
mistakesToAvoid: Only "handled recruitment" with no scale, tools, or outcomes.
Senior CV missing awareness of payroll, policies, or ER basics when role requires.
Copying sales revenue bullets that are not people metrics.
Overstating legal expertise — keep compliance as awareness/training.
atsIntro: Use titles and keywords aligned to the JD: Talent Acquisition, HR Business Partner, People Operations. Indian enterprises often scan for HRIS and payroll familiarity.
atsChecklist: Mention volume (hires/year) or team size when accurate.
Name HRIS/ATS you administered or used daily.
Include one metric on joiners, TAT, or engagement if available.
Keep POSH and labour references factual to your training or policy work — not legal claims.

title: BPO / Customer Service Resume Example
description: Voice and non-voice support resumes — languages, CSAT, SLAs, and clean ATS formatting for Indian BPO hiring.
sampleSummary: Customer service professional with 4 years in voice and chat support for telecom clients. Strong CSAT track record, adherence to schedules, and clear escalation discipline.
sampleBullets: Maintained CSAT of 4.6/5 across 1,200+ monthly interactions; met QA targets 11 months straight.
Handled billing and plan-change queries; reduced repeat contacts by confirming resolution steps.
Trained 8 new hires on CRM notes, de-escalation, and compliance scripts.
indiaContext: Indian BPO and GCC hiring emphasizes shift-ready reliability, language coverage (English plus Hindi or regional languages), and measurable quality—CSAT, QA, AHT—where available. Clear process type (voice vs chat) helps recruiters map you to the right queue.
aiEraNote: Chatbots and AI assist handles tier-1 queries — humans still own escalations, empathy, and quality scores. Your resume should highlight CRM, languages, and KPIs; "AI support" is relevant only for tech-support roles with real tool exposure.

earlyCareer: CRM case logging (Zendesk, Freshdesk, Salesforce Service)
Typing speed for chat; headset discipline for voice
senior: Team lead / SME tracks & training ownership

seniorityNote: Frontline resumes foreground languages, adherence, and CSAT/QA where permitted. Team leads and SMEs should add training batches, calibration participation, and measurable process fixes—keep metrics truthful to client policies.
tips: State process type: voice, chat, email, or blended; name domain if allowed (telecom, banking).
Metrics: CSAT, AHT, FCR, QA scores, attendance — pick what you truly have.
Languages: English + Hindi + regional languages are differentiators in India.
Mention shift flexibility or WFH stability if relevant.
Typing speed for chat roles when strong (e.g. 40+ WPM).
mistakesToAvoid: Heavy graphics or tables that break ATS on Naukri uploads.
Omitting languages when bilingual support is required.
Vague "customer handling" with no metrics or process detail.
Dumping IT keywords that do not match support roles.
atsIntro: BPO JDs use voice process, chat support, inbound, outbound, and SLA language. Match that vocabulary to your actual process.
atsChecklist: List languages with proficiency (read/write/speak) if space allows.
Include CSAT, QA, or productivity metric if permitted.
Keep layout single-column and plain.
Remove unrelated technical stacks unless you moved to tech support with real tools.

title: Accountant Resume Example
description: Qualification-forward layout for CA pipeline, commerce grads, and industry accountants — GST, Tally, and close process keywords.
sampleSummary: Semi-qualified CA (Group II cleared) with 2.5 years articleship in statutory audit and 1 year in industry accounts. Hands-on GST compliance, Tally ERP, and month-end close support.
sampleBullets: Supported statutory audit engagements for 6 manufacturing clients; led walkthroughs on revenue and inventory cycles.
Prepared GST returns and reconciliations; resolved mismatches with clear documentation for partner review.
Assisted month-end close: accruals, prepaid schedules, and bank reconciliations for ₹80Cr+ revenue entity.
indiaContext: Accountant and CA-pipeline hiring in India is credential-sensitive: articleship, group clearance, and GST/tally depth matter. Industry vs audit firm CVs use different proof—close cycles and compliance for industry; engagement scope and walkthroughs for practice.
aiEraNote: Excel copilots and tax software automation help — errors still land on you professionally. Lead with qualifications, ERP, and compliance depth; AI is optional colour only if your firm adopted specific tools you used.

earlyCareer: Tally / Zoho Books / busy tools from articleship
midLevel: SAP FI / MS Dynamics exposure when real
senior: Tax strategy support with CA sign-off context

seniorityNote: Articleship-heavy CVs stress audit cycles, walkthroughs, and GST/statutory rigour. Industry accountants foreground close calendars, MIS packs, and ERP depth; vocabulary should mirror the JD family—audit, tax, GL, or reporting—without claiming partner-level sign-offs you did not hold.
tips: Lead with qualification stage: CA final/inter, CMA, M.Com, B.Com — and articleship where relevant.
Separate audit firm experience from industry roles; scope each clearly.
Software: Tally, SAP FI, Zoho Books, advanced Excel — truthful depth only.
Bullets: close tasks, audits, tax, reconciliations — with materiality or entity scale if allowed.
FP&A vs pure accounting: use vocabulary matching the JD.
mistakesToAvoid: Generic "finance professional" with no credential or scope.
Missing articleship detail when applying for audit-heavy roles.
IFRS/Ind AS claims beyond your level.
Responsibility lists with no cycle, tool, or outcome.
atsIntro: Finance roles scan for GST, Tally, audit, reconciliation, and ERP keywords. Match the JD family: audit, tax, GL, or management reporting.
atsChecklist: State qualification status honestly (cleared groups, attempts if policy allows).
Name ERP/accounting software you used in production.
Include compliance or close-cycle terms from your real work.
Avoid unrelated corporate KPIs from non-finance roles.

title: Teacher / Educator Resume Example
description: Board-aware resume patterns for school teaching roles — subjects, grades, pedagogy, and institutional keywords.
sampleSummary: Secondary Mathematics teacher (grades 8–10) with 5 years in CBSE schools. Focus on concept clarity, formative assessment, and parent communication; comfortable integrating digital practice tools.
sampleBullets: Designed lesson sequences for algebra and geometry aligned to board pattern; improved class average scores year-on-year.
Led maths club and inter-school quiz prep; coordinated with parents on learning gaps and support plans.
Participated in NCERT workshop on competency-based assessment; piloted rubric-style grading in unit tests.
indiaContext: School hiring in India varies by board (CBSE, ICSE, State), medium, and institution type. JDs scan for subject mastery, grade band, B.Ed/TET status, and classroom management—keep tone formal and avoid unsubstantiated outcome claims.
aiEraNote: AI tutors and lesson generators can assist planning — schools still evaluate your pedagogy, classroom control, and assessment integrity. Mention LMS / smart-class tools only when you actively used them with students.

earlyCareer: Basic LMS / Google Classroom if deployed in school
midLevel: Digital worksheets, quiz tools (Kahoot, Quizizz) when school-approved
senior: Department lead, syllabus workshops, mentoring juniors

seniorityNote: Classroom teachers highlight subjects, grades, boards, and assessment methods parents and principals trust. Senior educators and coordinators add department initiatives, peer mentoring, and institutional programmes—keep student outcome claims ethical and verifiable.
tips: Open with subjects, grades, and boards taught (CBSE, ICSE, State).
Bullets: curriculum, assessment methods, parent engagement, co-curricular leadership.
Certifications: B.Ed, CTET/TET, subject diplomas — with year.
International schools: mention frameworks (IB PYP/MYP) only if experienced.
Keep tone formal; avoid long philosophy essays.
mistakesToAvoid: Missing board or syllabus clarity.
Informal tone or emojis unsuitable for institutional hiring.
Promising specific student grade outcomes unethically.
Long generic mission statements without teaching proof.
atsIntro: School JDs scan for board names, subjects, grades, B.Ed, and classroom keywords. Private schools may add digital literacy or co-curricular expectations.
atsChecklist: Name at least one board and the grade band you taught.
List primary subjects in skills or summary.
Include certifications required by the employer type.
Use school-appropriate language; avoid corporate sales metrics unless relevant (e.g. admissions in admin roles).
